---
title: "Armada EDA"
author: "Franco Ren"
date: "2025-09-17"
output:
  pdf_document: default
  html_document: default
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
library(tidyverse)
library(readxl)
library(janitor)
library(arrow)
library(lubridate)
library(dplyr)
library(scales)
library(lme4)
library(DHARMa)
library(broom.mixed)
```

```{r}
files <- list.files(pattern = "CMU_ARMADA_LOADS_202403-202502_.*\\.xlsx$")
files
```

```{r}
# read and append
dfs <- lapply(files, function(f) read_excel(f, guess_max = 100000))

# force them all to character to avoid type mismatch
dfs <- lapply(dfs, function(df) mutate_all(df, as.character))

# append into one big dataframe
combined <- bind_rows(dfs)

combined <- readr::type_convert(combined, na = c("", "NA", "N/A", "NULL"))

#names unified
combined <- janitor::clean_names(combined)

#choose numeric targets
num_cols <- c(
  "mileage","contract_linehaul","spot_linehaul","paid_linehaul","nap_linehaul",
  "cube","weight","pieces","quantity","transit_time","number_of_stops"
)

char_num_cols <- intersect(
  num_cols,
  names(combined)[vapply(combined, is.character, logical(1))]
)

combined <- combined %>%
  mutate(across(all_of(char_num_cols), readr::parse_number))
```
```{r}
#parse month from TIME_PERIOD
combined <- combined %>%
  mutate(
    time_period = as.character(time_period),
    month_date  = lubridate::parse_date_time(time_period, orders = c("b_Y","B_Y")),
    month       = lubridate::floor_date(month_date, "month")
  )
```

```{r}
# Loads by month
monthly_loads <- combined %>%
  count(month, name = "loads") %>%
  arrange(month) %>%
  mutate(month_label = format(month, "%b %Y"))

monthly_loads

monthly_loads <- monthly_loads %>%
  mutate(month = as.Date(month))

ggplot(monthly_loads, aes(x = month, y = loads)) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 1, color = "steelblue") +
  geom_point(color = "darkred") +
  scale_x_date(date_breaks = "1 month", date_labels = "%b %Y", expand = c(0.01, 0.01)) +
  labs(title = "Monthly Load Volume", x = "Month", y = "Loads") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1))
```
```{r}
# Top 10 origin states
top_origin_states <- combined %>%
  count(originstate, name = "loads") %>%
  arrange(desc(loads)) %>%
  slice_head(n = 10)

# Top 10 destination states
top_dest_states <- combined %>%
  count(destinationstate, name = "loads") %>%
  arrange(desc(loads)) %>%
  slice_head(n = 10)

ggplot(top_origin_states,
       aes(x = reorder(originstate, loads), y = loads)) +
  geom_col(fill = "steelblue") +
  coord_flip() +
  labs(title = "Top 10 Origin States by Load Volume",
       x = "Origin State",
       y = "Number of Loads") +
  theme_minimal()

ggplot(top_dest_states,
       aes(x = reorder(destinationstate, loads), y = loads)) +
  geom_col(fill = "darkorange") +
  coord_flip() +
  labs(title = "Top 10 Destination States by Load Volume",
       x = "Destination State",
       y = "Number of Loads") +
  theme_minimal()
```
```{r}
library(dplyr)
library(lubridate)
library(ggplot2)
library(forcats)
library(tidyr)

#Define the trusted window
start_date <- as.Date("2024-03-01")
end_date   <- as.Date("2025-02-28")

# Convert date columns
date_cols <- c(
  "pick_due_date","pick_departure_date","pick_actual_date","pick_appt_date",
  "drop_due_date","drop_departure_date","drop_actual_date","drop_appt_date"
)
combined <- combined %>%
  mutate(across(all_of(intersect(names(.), date_cols)), as.Date))

# Filter to the analysis window using pick_actual_date
df <- combined %>%
  filter(!is.na(pick_actual_date),
         pick_actual_date >= start_date,
         pick_actual_date <= end_date) %>%
  mutate(
    month = floor_date(pick_actual_date, "month"),
    week  = floor_date(pick_actual_date, "week"),
    # timing features
    transit_days = as.numeric(drop_actual_date - pick_actual_date),
    pick_delay_d = as.numeric(pick_actual_date - pick_appt_date),
    drop_delay_d = as.numeric(drop_actual_date - drop_appt_date)
  )

#missing %
date_missing <- df %>% summarise(across(all_of(date_cols), ~mean(is.na(.))))
print(t(date_missing))

date_ranges <- df %>% summarise(across(all_of(date_cols),
                                       ~paste0(as.character(min(., na.rm=TRUE)),
                                               " → ",
                                               as.character(max(., na.rm=TRUE)))))
print(t(date_ranges))

on_time <- c(
  on_time_pick = mean(coalesce(df$pick_delay_d <= 0, FALSE)),
  on_time_drop = mean(coalesce(df$drop_delay_d <= 0, FALSE))
)
print(on_time)

# Clip outliers (1–99% quantiles)
q <- function(x, p) suppressWarnings(quantile(x, probs = p, na.rm = TRUE))
pick_lo <- q(df$pick_delay_d, 0.01); pick_hi <- q(df$pick_delay_d, 0.99)
drop_lo <- q(df$drop_delay_d, 0.01); drop_hi <- q(df$drop_delay_d, 0.99)
tran_lo <- q(df$transit_days, 0.01); tran_hi <- q(df$transit_days, 0.99)

df <- df %>%
  mutate(
    pick_delay_clip = pmin(pmax(pick_delay_d, pick_lo),  pick_hi),
    drop_delay_clip = pmin(pmax(drop_delay_d, drop_lo),  drop_hi),
    transit_clip    = pmin(pmax(transit_days, tran_lo),  tran_hi)
  )

# Time series: Monthly & Weekly Loads
monthly_loads <- df %>% count(month, name = "loads") %>% arrange(month)
weekly_loads  <- df %>% count(week,  name = "loads") %>% arrange(week)

ggplot(monthly_loads, aes(month, loads)) +
  geom_col() +
  scale_x_date(limits = c(start_date, end_date),
               date_breaks = "1 month", date_labels = "%b %Y", expand = c(0,0)) +
  labs(title = "Monthly Pickups", x = "Month", y = "Loads") +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1))

ggplot(weekly_loads, aes(week, loads)) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 1) + geom_point(size = 1.4) +
  scale_x_date(limits = c(start_date, end_date),
               date_breaks = "1 month", date_labels = "%b %Y") +
  labs(title = "Weekly Pickups", x = "Week", y = "Loads") +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1))

# Delay & Transit distributions (clipped)
ggplot(df, aes(pick_delay_clip)) +
  geom_histogram(binwidth = 1) +
  labs(title = "Pickup Delay (days, clipped 1–99%)",
       x = "Days (Actual - Appt)", y = "Loads")

ggplot(df, aes(drop_delay_clip)) +
  geom_histogram(binwidth = 1) +
  labs(title = "Drop Delay (days, clipped 1–99%)",
       x = "Days (Actual - Appt)", y = "Loads")

ggplot(df, aes(transit_clip)) +
  geom_histogram(binwidth = 1) +
  labs(title = "Transit Time (days, clipped 1–99%)",
       x = "Days", y = "Loads")

# lane heatmap
top_orig <- df %>% count(originstate, sort = TRUE) %>% slice_head(n = 12) %>% pull(originstate)
top_dest <- df %>% count(destinationstate, sort = TRUE) %>% slice_head(n = 12) %>% pull(destinationstate)

flow_heat <- df %>%
  filter(originstate %in% top_orig, destinationstate %in% top_dest) %>%
  count(originstate, destinationstate, name = "loads") %>%
  mutate(
    originstate      = fct_reorder(originstate,      loads, .fun = sum),
    destinationstate = fct_reorder(destinationstate, loads, .fun = sum)
  )

ggplot(flow_heat, aes(originstate, destinationstate, fill = loads)) +
  geom_tile() +
  scale_fill_continuous(trans = "log10") +
  labs(title = "Top State↔State Lanes (log scale)",
       x = "Origin", y = "Destination", fill = "Loads") +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 90, vjust = 0.5))

#all states
flow_heat_all <- df %>%
  count(originstate, destinationstate, name = "loads") %>%
  filter(!is.na(originstate), !is.na(destinationstate))

ggplot(flow_heat_all, aes(originstate, destinationstate, fill = loads)) +
  geom_tile() +
  scale_fill_continuous(trans = "log10") +   # log scale keeps extreme lanes visible
  labs(title = "All State↔State Lanes (log scale)",
       x = "Origin", y = "Destination", fill = "Loads") +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 90, vjust = 0.5))

# Top-25 lanes table 
lane_counts <- df %>%
  count(originstate, destinationstate, name = "loads") %>%
  arrange(desc(loads)) %>% slice_head(n = 25)
lane_counts

# Top origins and destinations
top_origins <- combined %>%
  count(originid, sort = TRUE) %>%
  slice_head(n = 20)

top_destinations <- combined %>%
  count(destinationid, sort = TRUE) %>%
  slice_head(n = 20)

print(top_origins)
print(top_destinations)

# Distribution plots
ggplot(top_origins, aes(x = reorder(originid, n), y = n)) +
  geom_col(fill = "steelblue") +
  coord_flip() +
  labs(title = "Top 20 Origin IDs", x = "Origin ID", y = "Loads")

ggplot(top_destinations, aes(x = reorder(destinationid, n), y = n)) +
  geom_col(fill = "darkorange") +
  coord_flip() +
  labs(title = "Top 20 Destination IDs", x = "Destination ID", y = "Loads")

# Lane-level counts (origin to destination pairs)
lane_counts <- combined %>%
  count(originid, destinationid, sort = TRUE)

head(lane_counts, 20)

# Heatmap of top lanes
lane_top <- lane_counts %>%
  slice_head(n = 50)   # adjust number if want more

ggplot(lane_top, aes(x = originid, y = destinationid, fill = n)) +
  geom_tile() +
  scale_fill_viridis_c(trans = "log10") +
  labs(title = "Top Origin-Destination Lanes (log scale)", fill = "Loads") +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 90, vjust = 0.5))

```

```{r}
top_customers <- combined %>% 
  count(customer_skey, sort = TRUE) %>% 
  slice_head(n = 10)

top_customers

top_carriers <- combined %>% 
  count(carrier_skey, sort = TRUE) %>% 
  slice_head(n = 10)

top_carriers

combined %>% 
  count(destination_region) %>%    
  filter(n > 10) %>%           
  summarise(num_regions = n()) 
```

```{r}
df <- df %>%
  mutate(
    rate_premium = ifelse(
      !is.na(spot_linehaul) & spot_linehaul > 0,
      100 * (paid_linehaul - spot_linehaul) / spot_linehaul,
      NA_real_
    )
  )
summary(df$rate_premium)

# check missingness and distribution by award type
df %>%
  group_by(award_type) %>%
  summarise(
    n = n(),
    missing = mean(is.na(rate_premium)),
    avg_premium = mean(rate_premium, na.rm = TRUE),
    median_premium = median(rate_premium, na.rm = TRUE)
  )


market_premium <- df %>%
  filter(!is.na(originstate)) %>% 
  group_by(originstate) %>%
  summarise(avg_premium = mean(rate_premium, na.rm = TRUE)) %>%
  arrange(desc(avg_premium))

```
```{r}
ggplot(market_premium,
       aes(x = reorder(originstate, avg_premium), y = avg_premium)) +
  geom_col(fill = "darkorange") +
  coord_flip() +
  labs(
    title = "Origin States by Average Rate Premium",
    x = "Origin State",
    y = "Avg Premium (%)"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()
```

```{r}
dest_premium <- df %>%
  filter(!is.na(destinationstate)) %>% # drop missing values
  group_by(destinationstate) %>%
  summarise(avg_premium = mean(rate_premium, na.rm = TRUE)) %>%
```


```{r}
arrange(desc(avg_premium))

ggplot(dest_premium,
       aes(x = reorder(destinationstate, avg_premium), y = avg_premium)) +
  geom_col(fill = "darkorange") +
  coord_flip() +
  labs(
    title = "Destination States by Average Rate Premium",
    x = "Destination State",
    y = "Avg Premium (%)"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()
```
```{r}
inbound <- df %>%
  filter(!is.na(destinationstate)) %>%
  group_by(destinationstate) %>%
  summarise(inbound_loads = n())

outbound <- df %>%
  filter(!is.na(originstate)) %>%
  group_by(originstate) %>%
  summarise(outbound_loads = n())

flow_balance <- full_join(outbound, inbound,
                          by = c("originstate" = "destinationstate")) %>%
  rename(market = originstate) %>%
  mutate(
    inbound_loads  = coalesce(inbound_loads, 0),
    outbound_loads = coalesce(outbound_loads, 0),
    total_loads    = inbound_loads + outbound_loads
  )

flow_balance <- flow_balance %>%
  mutate(
    in_out_ratio = ifelse(outbound_loads > 0,
                          inbound_loads / outbound_loads, NA_real_),
    balance_type = case_when(
      in_out_ratio < 0.8 ~ "Headhaul",
      in_out_ratio > 1.2 ~ "Deadhaul",
      TRUE               ~ "Balanced"
    )
  )

market_premium <- df %>%
  filter(!is.na(originstate)) %>%
  group_by(originstate) %>%
  summarise(avg_premium = mean(rate_premium, na.rm = TRUE))

# Join
flow_premium <- flow_balance %>%
  left_join(market_premium, by = c("market" = "originstate"))

ggplot(flow_premium, aes(x = balance_type, y = avg_premium, fill = balance_type)) +
  geom_boxplot(outlier.alpha = 0.2) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("Headhaul" = "#1b9e77", 
                               "Balanced" = "#7570b3", 
                               "Deadhaul" = "#d95f02")) +
  labs(
    title = "Rate Premium by Market Balance Type",
    x = "Market Type",
    y = "Average Rate Premium (%)"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()

fb_us <- flow_balance %>%
  mutate(market = toupper(market)) %>%
  filter(market %in% usmap::statepop$abbr) %>%         # U.S. only
  mutate(balance_type = recode(balance_type, "Deadhaul" = "Backhaul (Deadhaul)"))

headhaul_states  <- fb_us %>% filter(balance_type == "Headhaul")            %>% arrange(desc(in_out_ratio)) %>% pull(market)
backhaul_states  <- fb_us %>% filter(balance_type == "Backhaul (Deadhaul)") %>% arrange(desc(in_out_ratio)) %>% pull(market)
balanced_states  <- fb_us %>% filter(balance_type == "Balanced")            %>% arrange(desc(in_out_ratio)) %>% pull(market)

headhaul_states
backhaul_states
balanced_states

```

```{r}
library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(usmap)
library(forcats)

flow_balance <- flow_balance %>%
  mutate(market = toupper(market))

flow_balance_map <- flow_balance %>%
  left_join(usmap::statepop %>% select(fips, abbr),
            by = c("market" = "abbr"))

us_abbr <- usmap::statepop$abbr  #US state codes

flow_us <- flow_balance_map %>%
  filter(market %in% us_abbr) # keep only US rows

plot_usmap(
  data    = flow_us,
  values  = "balance_type",
  regions = "states"
) +
  scale_fill_manual(
    values = c("Headhaul" = "darkblue",
               "Balanced" = "#BDBDBD",
               "Deadhaul" = "darkorange",
               "NA" = "#EEEEEE"),
    na.value = "grey90",
    name = "Market type"
  ) +
  labs(title = "Market Balance by State (Inbound/Outbound Ratio)",
       subtitle = "Headhaul (<0.8), Balanced (0.8–1.2), Backhaul (>1.2)") +
  theme(legend.position = "right")
```
```{r}

```

```{r}
library(dplyr)
library(lubridate)

min_loads <- 50  # filter tiny markets so stats are stable

market_o <- df %>%
  mutate(month = floor_date(pick_actual_date, "month"),
         rate_premium = ifelse(spot_linehaul > 0,
                               100*(paid_linehaul - spot_linehaul)/spot_linehaul,
                               NA_real_)) %>%
  filter(!is.na(originstate)) %>%
  group_by(originstate) %>%
  summarise(
    loads                = n(),
    uniq_carriers        = n_distinct(carrier_skey),
    # a repeat carrier handled >= 3 loads in this market
    repeat_carriers      = n_distinct(carrier_skey[carrier_skey %in%
                                   names(which(table(carrier_skey) >= 3))]),
    months_active        = n_distinct(month),
    loads_per_carrier    = loads / pmax(uniq_carriers, 1),
    carriers_per_100L    = 100 * uniq_carriers / pmax(loads,1),
    repeat_share         = repeat_carriers / pmax(uniq_carriers,1),
    prem_mean            = mean(rate_premium, na.rm = TRUE),
    prem_sd              = sd(rate_premium, na.rm = TRUE),
    mileage_med          = median(mileage, na.rm = TRUE),
    transit_med          = median(as.numeric(drop_actual_date - pick_actual_date), na.rm = TRUE),
    leadtime_med         = median(load_lead_time, na.rm = TRUE),
    stops_med            = median(number_of_stops, na.rm = TRUE)
  ) %>%
  filter(loads >= min_loads) %>%
  ungroup()

# Does higher premium come with lower engagement?
cor.test(market_o$prem_mean, market_o$carriers_per_100L, method = "spearman")
cor.test(market_o$prem_mean, market_o$repeat_share,      method = "spearman")

# Scatter to eyeball the slope
library(ggplot2)
ggplot(market_o, aes(prem_mean, carriers_per_100L)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.6) + geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = FALSE) +
  labs(x = "Avg rate premium (%)", y = "Unique carriers per 100 loads")
```
```{r}
market_d <- df %>%
  filter(!is.na(destinationstate)) %>%
  group_by(destinationstate) %>%
  summarise(
    loads                = n(),
    uniq_carriers        = n_distinct(carrier_skey),
    months_active        = n_distinct(month),
    loads_per_carrier    = loads / pmax(uniq_carriers, 1),
    carriers_per_100L    = 100 * uniq_carriers / pmax(loads, 1),
    repeat_carriers      = n_distinct(carrier_skey[carrier_skey %in%
                                   names(which(table(carrier_skey) >= 3))]),
    repeat_share         = repeat_carriers / pmax(uniq_carriers, 1),
    prem_mean            = mean(rate_premium, na.rm = TRUE),
    prem_sd              = sd(rate_premium, na.rm = TRUE),
    mileage_med          = median(mileage, na.rm = TRUE),
    transit_med          = median(as.numeric(drop_actual_date - pick_actual_date), na.rm = TRUE),
    leadtime_med         = median(load_lead_time, na.rm = TRUE),
    stops_med            = median(number_of_stops, na.rm = TRUE)
  ) %>%
  filter(loads >= min_loads) %>% 
  ungroup()

# Spearman correlations
cor.test(market_d$prem_mean, market_d$carriers_per_100L, method = "spearman")
cor.test(market_d$prem_mean, market_d$repeat_share,      method = "spearman")

ggplot(market_d, aes(prem_mean, carriers_per_100L)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.6) +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = FALSE) +
  labs(x = "Avg rate premium (%)", y = "Unique carriers per 100 loads",
       title = "Destination markets: premium vs carrier breadth")

ggplot(market_d, aes(prem_mean, repeat_share)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.6) +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = FALSE) +
  labs(x = "Avg rate premium (%)", y = "Repeat-carrier share",
       title = "Destination markets: premium vs carrier stickiness")
```
```{r}
z <- function(x) as.numeric(scale(x))
market_d <- market_d %>%
  mutate(
    CAI_rule =  + z(carriers_per_100L)      # breadth
                + z(repeat_share)           # stickiness
                + z(loads_per_carrier)      # commitment
                - z(prem_mean)              # risk pricing
                - z(prem_sd)                # price volatility
                - z(mileage_med) - z(transit_med) - z(stops_med)
  ) %>%
  arrange(desc(CAI_rule))
```

```{r}
library(tidymodels)
set.seed(1)

market_d2 <- market_d %>%
  mutate(engagement = z(carriers_per_100L) + z(repeat_share) + z(loads_per_carrier)) %>%
  select(destinationstate, engagement,
         prem_mean, prem_sd, mileage_med, transit_med, leadtime_med, stops_med, loads)

spl <- initial_split(market_d2, prop=.8, strata=engagement)
tr <- training(spl); te <- testing(spl)

rec <- recipe(engagement ~ ., data=tr) %>%
  update_role(destinationstate, new_role="id") %>%
  step_zv(all_predictors()) %>%
  step_normalize(all_predictors())

mod <- linear_reg(penalty=tune(), mixture=tune()) %>% set_engine("glmnet")
wf  <- workflow() %>% add_recipe(rec) %>% add_model(mod)

vfold <- vfold_cv(tr, v=5)
grid  <- grid_regular(penalty(range=c(-6,0)), mixture(), levels=7)

res   <- tune_grid(wf, resamples=vfold, grid=grid, metrics=metric_set(rmse, rsq))
best <- select_best(res, metric = "rmse")
final <- finalize_workflow(wf, best) %>% fit(tr)

coefs <- broom::tidy(extract_fit_parsnip(final)$fit)   # learned weights
te_pred <- predict(final, new_data = te)
te <- bind_cols(te, te_pred)

collect_metrics(res)

coefs

te %>%
  arrange(desc(.pred)) %>%
  select(destinationstate, .pred) %>%
  head(10)

ggplot(te, aes(x = engagement, y = .pred)) +
  geom_point() +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = FALSE, color = "blue") +
  labs(title = "Predicted vs Actual Engagement (Elastic Net)",
       x = "Actual Engagement",
       y = "Predicted Engagement")

```
```{r}
df_clean <- df %>%
  filter(!is.na(award_type)) %>%
  mutate(award_group = ifelse(award_type == "Primary", "Primary", "Not Primary"))

df_prop <- df_clean %>%
  group_by(temperature_req, award_group) %>%
  summarise(count = n()) %>%
  group_by(temperature_req) %>%
  mutate(prop = count / sum(count))

ggplot(df_prop, aes(x = temperature_req, y = prop, fill = award_group)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", position = "fill") +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = percent_format()) +
  labs(
    title = "Proportion of Primary vs Non-Primary Loads by Temperature Requirement",
    x = "Temperature Requirement",
    y = "Proportion of Loads",
    fill = "Award Type"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1))
```
```{r}
df_price <- df_bin %>%
  mutate(rate_premium = (paid_linehaul - spot_linehaul) / pmax(spot_linehaul, 1e-6))

df_price %>%
  group_by(primary) %>%
  summarise(n=n(), prem_mean = mean(rate_premium, na.rm=TRUE), prem_med = median(rate_premium, na.rm=TRUE), prem_sd = sd(rate_premium, na.rm=TRUE))

ggplot(df_price, aes(x = primary, y = rate_premium)) +
  geom_boxplot(outlier.alpha = .3) +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = percent) +
  labs(x="Primary?", y="Rate premium")
```
```{r}
df1 <- df %>%
  filter(!is.na(award_type),
         mileage > 0,
         paid_linehaul > 0) %>%
  mutate(
    award_group   = ifelse(award_type == "Primary", "Primary", "Not Primary"),
    rate_per_mile = paid_linehaul / mileage
  )

ggplot(df1, aes(x = award_group, y = rate_per_mile, fill = award_group)) +
  geom_boxplot(outlier.alpha = 0.25) +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = dollar_format()) +
  labs(title = "Rate per mile by Award Type",
       x = NULL, y = "Paid $/mile") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
  theme(legend.position = "none")

df1 %>%
  group_by(award_group) %>%
  summarise(n = n(),
            mean_rpm = mean(rate_per_mile, na.rm = TRUE),
            sd_rpm = sd(rate_per_mile, na.rm = TRUE)) %>%
  mutate(mean_rpm = dollar(mean_rpm))

t.test(rate_per_mile ~ award_group, data = df1)
```
